title: pelargonium × flabellifolium
intro: Pelargonium × flabellifolium, also known as the Cape Geranium or Fish River Geranium, is a species of flowering plant that belongs to the Pelargonium genus and is a hybrid of Pelargonium betulinum and Pelargonium multilaciniatum. This plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, particularly its ability to exhibit antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory effects. The health benefits of Pelargonium × flabellifolium include its potential to alleviate respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma, as well as its ability to soothe digestive problems and reduce stress. The herbal preparations of this plant can be consumed as teas, infusions, or capsules, and it is also used in aromatherapy due to its calming scent.
